Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0xC900 "NUCLEARWARGAMES-C900" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Nuclear War Games.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
This game has a BASIC intro before the main BASIC game loads. However, as the main game program overwrites this when loaded, the following steps were taken to retain the intro:
1. The SYS49194 call on line 412 in the BASIC intro was changed to a SYS call to a new machine code routine at $CA00. 2. The memory from $0800 to $2EFF ($2700 bytes) was dumped, which includes the BASIC intro and data. 3. After the actual BASIC game has finished loading, the memory occupying the 9984 ($2700) bytes at $0800 was copied to $D000. 4. The data dumped in step 2 was restored to $0800. 5. A new routine was placed at $C900 which sets up the correct VICII settings and necessary BASIC pointers and runs the intro program. 6. This routine also disables the RUN/STOP key by modifying the STOP vector by setting $FE at $0328. 7. Once the intro program has finished, the added SYS call jumps to the new routine at $CA00, which restores the 9984 ($2700) bytes of data from $D000 back to $0800 before setting the correct VICII settings and necessary BASIC pointers for the game itself and runs it.
Note: the memory between $9620-$BFFF and $C900-$CFFF contained an unecessary repeating memory block. Therefore these ranges were zeroed out.
